text: I had mine 6 months ago and now my pain in worse. anyone else?

would an mri scan pick up endometriosis elsewhere in the body? It has been detected on my pouch of Douglas but they only did a pelvic mri, would it be beneficial to request further investigations? Ty 🎗
I wish I would of knew to ask I ended up needed to get two laparoscopy surgeries
what if you ask for a 360 an they say no are they allowed to say no?
Mine refused to check my bowel .
also if you have deep tissue endo it wont show. only a mri will show it!! (that's what happend to me. thankfully I didnt get the surgery in the end!)
I have stage 4 endo when I have my laparoscopy they want to do a full hysterectomy
The pouch of who? 😳😂

question why is nobody doing MRIs? i had one done in Russia electively just to check if my lap surgery in Ireland missed any (they didn't thankfully), confirmed adenomyosis, checked the rest, all for an hour of my time and €100 privately. why is it not an option in Ireland?
I got told surgeon only focuses on pelvis and stomach area....
At this point I’m going to get a degree in medicine and do it myself 😐
I had a gynaecologist and she found endo and adenomyosis last week. They removed the endo and have now asked if id like to try Dienogest before deciding if I want a hysterectomy. I’m 40.
I’m actually just going to play this video for my doctor. lol
can u request this in the ER
is the pouch of Douglas the same as a posterior cul-de-sac?
What do you mean about excision not ablation? (not sure how to spell it sorry English is not my first language)

Wow, we are so moved by the response to this video! Thank you to everyone sharing their own stories and helping us reach more people who need to watch this.
We’ll be posting more from our interviews with Liz soon, so stay tuned. For those asking, Liz is an Endometriosis Clinical Nurse Specialist based in Wales who provides private virtual consultations. If you’re looking to connect with her, let us know and we can help.
This is liz bruen! She was one of the first people to push for my endo diagnosis! She and dr Anthony Griffiths are two of the most incredible, talented and gifted specialists in this field! ♥️♥️
This is very helpful thankyou!, I have my surgery on the 11th May should I ask for this on the day or would it be best if I sent them an email beforehand? x
shouldn't they know this. why do we have to tell them
I am so happy my DR DID IT RIGHT 😭
This is the lady who told me I had endometriosis after surgery... Incredible woman
I had my surgery with a general gynaecologist, was in surgery for 30 minutes, told there was no endo and pain is in my head. No photos either to take to a specialist.
I have a laparoscopy on the 6th May, should I email them beforehand?
I’m still so convinced my doctors missed endo, I don’t know that I can go through the mental process of requesting another one and if it doesn’t show it I’m lost again
Hi! I’m going in tomorrow & realise I know nothing about what’s going on. Is this something I can ask for when I meet the team before beforehand or should this have been a conversation before?

Oh god mine was stage 4 and in my pouch of Douglas as well. This was 2009. Had two children since and no issues (although also have PCOS and hashimotos so I didn’t get away from evvvverything!) - fyi I’m in New Zealand

can someone explain why excision not ablation. I thought I had excision but since then, in conversations, laparoscopic ablation had also been mentioned and now I'm confused and have no clue which one I actually had.
also, is it normal for my GP not to have any medical letters or images of my treatment/surgery with gynaecology? recently asked but was told they don't hold any of that information and to ask the hospital directly (which is fine), I just wonder if they don't have documents then are they aware of the care I've had?
I've been having worse and more frequent flare ups these 6 months post op than before😒
Mine was missed 3 times with the Lap. I was a late teen with stage 4 Enso and adenomyosis.
How do I see Liz please? Endo and Adeno here, feel like I’m lost in the system.
hi, i have umbilical endometriosis. its very rare. does she have any information or anything about it. im 6year in and got my first gynecologist appointment nxt month. xx
my first lap was a JOKE. Waiting on my referral so I can get a 2nd opinion & better care! 🤞🏼

My first diagnosis was stage 3 was 2023 last month march I want for Lapo endo surgery they said they didn’t see anything, I asked her did you check my bladder and rectal she said no . During recovery I have all endo symptoms and my Gp said I should keep record for my follow up. Right now my confused
in Canada and having surgery by an endometriosis specialist and nothing was offered for further investigation before surgery. I'm terrified.
Can you see this lady ? Get an appointment
@Abi for your most recent post💛
My doctor listened to my symptoms and said I need to do laparoscopic and thats the only way to discover it !
I had a laprascopy for Fibroid removal and my surgeon was so good she looked for and found endo!!
My appendix burst I think it’s from endo. So hard to get diagnosed.

I’m having mine Wednesday, no other tests done. Not scans nothing. Asked why and the surgeon said it was as id had a scan with the gynaecologist but surly they don’t look for endo? Is this normal? Thanks
Lizzie’s the loveliest person 🥰
i’ve had mri and ultra sound both came up clear so next step i’m considering the laparoscopy but im afraid they won’t find anything :/
I just learned that I have pouch of Douglas endo
thank you for this. iv saved this video for when I'm seen soon for an endometriosis review consultation
Saw liz many years ago, very knowledgeable and skilled person.
Get ready for the shoulder pain being more painful than the surgery itself
I wished you’d seen my daughter, she is in so much pain all the time
Like many, I had no idea that there are gynecologists that specialize in endo, so I had mine with a general one and never felt a difference
Recently had one but didn’t tell me where they looked , was not aware of this helpful information hope this reaches someone before they have it for a better outcome

I know my laparoscopy was thorough as they also discovered cirrhosis on my liver that I was completely unaware of - have since been diagnosed with a rare liver disorder that may not have been caught in time otherwise
sounds good but ... very unlikely they would take any notice of what we ask for, as the patient
I wish I would have known this after having 4 then years later a hysterectomy
I’ve had many. Some see all of it. Some see nothing. It’s exhausting. I refuse everything now

I had one, they took photos but they just told me I had ibs. Now going for a ct but that’s it
This is exactly what happened to me… endo on mri but they couldn’t find it in surgery 😂
This is such invaluable advice!! 🙏❤️Do you have any recommendations for how to advocate for yourself if surgeons refuse or dismiss the request?
liz is an absolute god send
Most of my endo was in my pouch of Douglas
My doctor checked everything and found Adenomyosis
i have hard palpable lumps in the left of my pelvic floor. can endo feel hard on palpation?
I lucked out with my surgeon!! 💗 she found so much.

I've never grabbed a pen and paper faster. I feel like I'm getting an honorary med degree finding all of this out about my endo

Thank you!
thank you queen ❤️

Wow...

i did a mri which didn't show anything and she said it doesn't mean i don't have it but that what i have might be too small to show and that so a laparoscopy would be useless cause it is too small anyways
An ob-gyn diagnosed me with pelvic endometriosis and adenomyosis thru TVS. I was not advised to do laparoscopy.
Anyone have a good endo surgeon in NJ or NY?
Too late. Oh and they didn’t see anything
Check out www.heranova.com. The company just launched the its first non invasive endometriosis rule in test! Early detection is very important because it can come back.

you need to do it soon after period after is useful the assessment and actually procedure right ? thanks

I had a this surgery last month and they found nothing, which really shocked me as I was adamant something would be found x

The missed mine

I was promised a specialist and ended up with a general gyne and was told I had nothing m, no images were taken of the area where the mri showed endo … I don’t even think they checked behind my womb 😅 now I’m on the waiting list again
I asked and I was declined for a thoracic check 😭
wasn't there a conclusion that Laproscopic was far less successful than a full on open surgery as far as the condition worsening vs being better treated down the road?
Dose endo in the POD get worse over time ?

I had pouch of Douglas endo and it was so painful

Thank you for sharing this. I had one 30 years ago and it showed stage 4 Endo, Finroids and PCOS, a mega enlarged ureter and one kidney not fully functioning. Unfortunately they missed a bowel blockage with endometriosis and pouch of Douglas which 10 years later I had to have a resection due to complete blockage. It would have saved a lot of pain and huge health challenges if it was discovered then. I am now 56, full HRT, no babies ..

Same 🫠 going through all that made me feel so out of control… pain, heaviness, low energy all the time. I tried so many things and still felt stuck. A friend in a forum mentioned Eat Like a Woman Protocol written by loren green. I followed it and everything shifted—30 lbs gone, no more bloat, clearer mind, steady energy. kinda wild this isn’t talked about more, love it)
I just had a diagnostic one due to having all the symptoms and all I got told after was we done the surgery and we didn’t find anything and that was it I never saw them again
My laparoscopy ruined my body and I never even ended up getting results. I wish I'd known to ask for this
This lady was part of the surgical team when I had mine removed back in 2018! Very informative & thorough. They had me on the table for 7 hours, that’s how thorough they were & how bad mine was!

when I was 19 scans coming up clear then after 2 laps he found I was " as bad as someone with a few cesarean sections " kept asking how many kids I had . I was stuck back to front everywhere . doctor has no record of any of this
help me. I have a lump between my uterus and anus. no help just agonising pain in that area. I have all of the mri scans
Mine was missed. Diagnosed 4 years later from ultrasound. Appears to be DIE

This!!!! So may general gynaecologist miss it cause the don’t actually know what they are looking for. It’s ridiculous. They need to all go back to uni and do more research and study it more. This is why anyone trying to get a diagnosis needs to see a endo specalist not a general gynaecologist x

Also excision not ablation surgery - I had it round my Left ovary, Left sacral ligament, Pouch of Douglas & both Pelvic side walls. Best treatments I’ve had - 1) temporary menopause 2) dienogest. Both completely life changing.
I’ve had two ablations done and now FINALLY they are doing an excision!

Indeed👏🏻 👏🏻 They found more endometriosis by me with laparoscopie then by ultrasound. Iam gonna see the pictures 21 May.
Iam Only sad they did not nothing with the adenomyosis. 😔
i’m a month post op, told her all my symptoms led to deep infiltrating endometriosis, and she’s told me she looked at my ovaries, pouch of douglas, and uterus.
I’ve had a laparoscopy and was told nothing was found. I’m convinced it’s on my bowel and bladder. The only thing as my womb is inflamed but no more info. I don’t know what is wrong with me
Going through it all again at 70 despite a bowel resection and having endo in pouch if Douglas. Bladder was attached to bowel. May be adhesions but going through tests again.
I have all my images but no way to understand them. I have issues with my POD and ovary. As well as a 4.5 x 3.5 x 3.5 fibroid and I'm multi fibroid with bulky uterus. Confirmed endo
You’re telling me they can do surgeries on you and miss the actual thing and be like “you don’t have it” when you actually do 😳

Had a laproscopy done 07/2025 (ablation) "removed" the endo 10/2026 was told it "grew" bck.. 05/2026 i now have stage 4 deep infilteration 6.7cm chocolate cyst type endo which is effectin my bowels, my bladder & nerves in my upper thigh area. I really thnk my dr missed it da 1st time 😩 finding a good specialist who knws about this is really hard in FL

I had two laparoscopy surgeries, was told both times that I didn't have endo. They removed one of my fallopian tubes because it was twisted around my ovarie. Was told I'd never be able to conceive naturally. A year after I did infact fall pregnant naturally, I went for an early internal scan. The nurse that scanned me internally, looked at my scan for no longer than 60 seconds and told me I have endometrial tissue growing outside of my uterus. 60 seconds of looking at scan!!!

That's exactly what happened to me, the first laparoscopy I had, the non specialist Gyno performed the op, and told me he had found "a couple of spots, and performed ablation on them". Pain was still severe afterwards. Was lucky enough to have another laparoscopy (investigative one), by a specialist, he told me I had one of the worst case he had seen. Booked surgery, it was a 6 hour op with him AND a colorectal surgeon, they performed excision.

mine was just f-ing EVERYWHERE, at 17 after having my period officially for 6 months it was so bad. endo and pcos girl sending love to all the other warrior woman out there...due for yet another revisional layering and ovarian drilling...im so lucky compared to some, many are much worse. no blood transfusions or organs removed for over 3 years now...thats a huge win!!
If I’d have known this years ago! Two surgeries later , bowel resection and now due RATs for thoracic x
they wont listen. the docs dont care!!!!

why is it not mandatory for surgeons to do this rather than us expecting patients to use medical terminology they haven’t been trained in, and request techniques that the surgeon should already know? we need to get serious about actually reforming the system and ensuring doctors know what to do, of course for now it’s great to give patients the info, but we shouldn’t have to fight and request all these things that should just be the bare minimum of any endo surgery. they never checked anywhere higher than my pelvis during my surgery even though i asked them to check my bowel and appendix, my right upper quadrant tenderness and pain still remains. they also did ablation after my surgeon told me he was going to do excision. thankfully i was very lucky despite those things and my pain hugely improved after my surgery, but a lot of women will not have that same outcome, and we cannot afford for surgeons to ‘get it wrong’ and risk complications and scar tissue with repeated poorly performed surgeries.
Mine gynaecologist is on the nhs and she is bloody amazing

Had a laparoscopy in 2024. Was supposed to be diagnostic and then detach my ovaries etc. Wasn’t told that I had endo just that my uterus, ovaries, bowel and bladder were stuck together and that I had cysts that they dispersed and performed adhesiolysis but they did not look around to see how bad it was. This year found out I have stage3/4 endo, adenomyosis, I had endometriomas and still have one on my ovary. I have endo on my colon and deeply invasive endo of the posterior pelvis and diffuse adenomyosis. I am at a loss as to the next steps now

I have endometriosis pouch of Douglas, so I’m guessing they must of missed on my bowels?? I had my surgery in 2023, had a ct scan (I know isn’t great for endometriosis) but it showed a cyst on my ovaries, caused by the endometriosis. Does this mean it’s spread?

mine checked everything. ive now also got adenomyosis on top of endometriosis and i had my left ovary removed. im day 13 recovering and still very sore. but positivity is 70% this and every battles most important factor. im not dying it just feels it. so thats a blessing. mind over matter
Wish I knew this before I had mine done. They left so much scar tissue it made it worse after.
Day 3 of recovery since my Laproscopy I was supposed to have Excision and Ablation as well... he said nothing was found so nothing was removed and no samples were sent for testing. 😭
I was told they couldn't remove the endo near my rectum because it was too close to my rectum so they left it... is this something to look into??

my endo goes from my liver to my Sigmund colon, i know its coming back already in December I had a hysterectomy with tons endo and adhesions removed , they left my ovaries but the right one is twisted and adhered to my wall and im begging to have it removed because it hurts . I know I have more hiding because when they took my uterus out the found a turn in my intestines and I have a feeling that why I have to twist and such just to poop and it hurts
I saw this too late :( I had my surgery this morning. They did find a bunch of small lesions all over in my abdomen that they took samples of because it’s probably endo. Thank you for your content!!
who are we telling this to... Gyn, PCP, ER, Gastro , Neuro, Urology.....
This might be a dumb question but why is there a pouch named after someone called Douglas in a womens body?
My pouch of Douglas is (as they called it) obliterated😗✌🏽
I had surgery and the removed endometriosis from my ovaries, uterus, bowel and bladder
I had 1 as well my ovaries were opened as well 😳

I dont enjoy the level of disease i have but in a sense i am "lucky" to have 9cm of cysts having a party with my left ovary wearing my fallopian tube like a scarf in my pouch of Douglas, hard to miss :>
my endo had grown from my ovary to my pouch of Douglas I just had a MRI as still got pain x
why not ablation?

@m ☆: honestly this is kinda off topic but i feel like this is humanity de-evolving with conditions such as PCOS/Endo/Ando etc. because we were meant to be a matriarchy as humanity. human women go through menopause (check out the Grandma effect), and in nature we see the handful of animals that go through menopause too are matriarchal. this is natures way of telling us humans that we’ve strayed too far in terms of the natural order; i.e. the patriarchy. regardless, i’m sending my love to every women and girl going through these issues. :(🩷
My pouch of Douglas was obliterated by endo. Completely. Nothing left. 🥺

Actually I did the surgery 3 time for it to work ! The third time went very well cause the doctor was an endometriosis specialist… and I had to remove my R ovary to feel better ❤️‍🩹 ! GOOD LUCK TO ALL THE ENDO GIRLS OUT THERE ! Keep fighting

I had a laparscopy back in December... she found I had adhesions on my bowel, bowel was adhered to my ovary and my ovary adhered to my pelvic wall. She managed to release some bowel adhesions but couldn't release my ovary as it there was a risk of damaging my utera. No active endo was found but did see pelvic scarring. I'm still in a lot of pain around the pelvic area.... at my follow up appt, she downplayed my pain and told me that it doesn't really make much difference if my ovary is adhered to my pelvic wall and that it shouldn't really be causing me pain so I've just gotta lose 2/3stone and then she will think about putting me on another laparscopy wait list. 😳 Meanwhile I'm still more or less housebound due to my pain. This was a general gynocologist but does have interest in endometriosis and pelvic pain. I just don't know what to do now
endo mainly gets worse through diet wheat seed oils and cane sugar cut out will improve your endo and over all wellbeing .moringa herb 🌿 will help balance as does macca and wild yam 🍠

I lad a laparoscopy and the consultant said afterwards I have “scar tissue” where my left fallopian tube should be. And told me it had been removed. I’ve never had surgery besides a c section which I got my hospital notes that stated I had two healthy ones and they were not removed at that time. Any suggestions what I could do next to check? Was it the wrong report he was looking at? Or can they just disappear

Was told I was just being dramatic and it was just period. I kept pushing for a laproscopy, it took 6 years but they did it and found stage 4 endometriosis, full organ fusion of my uterus, bowel, bladder, kidney. it was growing up my spine and around into the back of my pelvis. bladder damage, fibroids, cysts, tumours and currently waiting for pathology for cancer. had a 3 week post surgery scan. Signs that the endo already grown back around my left side of my uterus and ovary, and my other ovary has grown a 4cm tumour in 3 weeks. They told me this morning, they aren't going to be able to stop it and they just want to make me comfortable till the "inevitable" hysterectomy will have to happen. they never wanted to do the surgery in the first place. I was told I was crazy.
I had surgical emphysema twice, then full hysterectomy at 31 and both ovaries removed.
What happens if they go in and find nothing… could this damage the womb?
Doctors don't listen
I was diagnosed with endo in 2022 by laparoscopy, had another one in 2025 and they said they could not find any, I don’t know what to do as I know it does not just dissapear
Can you ask for this in Scotland because every time i mention things Iv seen from specialists on Tiktok it seems like I have 2 heads🙈
That’s exactly what happened to me my gyno said I almost gave up and thought why is she in so much pain, and then he started moving around behind my bowel and loads were hiding behind there
yes I had this and they showed me pics of everywhere
I had lesions on my uterus but they just said it’s nothing and if I had pain carrying on the one would look 😅
I didn’t get a 360 an wish I did now 😖 I have bowel involvement but wasn’t told wether it was removed or not 💀

what if you have had two laparoscopy already, they said I have pelvic congestion syndrome.
I don't know if they looked for adenomyosis
the second one was p
because they perforated my uterus when they inserted a mirena coil and it ended up in the pouch of doglous and the coil had wrapped around my left fallopian tube. would they have found Endo then aswell? I'm. just not convinced I don't trust any of them anymore

I had laparoscopic surgery last month and still healing after a Hydrosalpinx fallopian tube removed on the right and diagnosed with stage 1 endo with slight damage to my left with slow flow. is it a risk to have another surgery? I’m trying to conceive prior trying ivf and I’m 39 only just been diagnosed when I’ve wanted answers for years 😫😭
Had mine removed. Turns out they missed loads. Need surgery again ^.^

I had a total of 5 surgeries for endometriosis, done in 3 different countries, I.e Italy the 1st, UK the 2nd, Romania the 3rd and the last 2 (the last was an emergency because of complications from previous) done again in UK by what I believed to be top endo specialists. Not once in the 12 years of me seeing dr after dr, have i heard of the things you mentioned here..... not once. I've been looking for a medic like yourself for so long .. if only I'd of found you in time 😭 it's too late now, I'm butchered in ways you can't imagine 😭

I wish I had seen this before my lap surgery 😫 I had it 9 months ago. my surgeon said he thinks I may have adenomyosis so I have an mri scan of my pelvis today. if it is seen then I have a choice of ablation or a hysterectomy 🤷‍♀️

Wish I had known this, I had surgery he looked in my uterus that was it and told me I hadn’t, I still have all the symptoms and was discharged by gyni and told sometimes people have pain that can’t be explained.

So we got to go tell the surgeon to do something they clearly have never done before? Especially as we have to tell them ? No thanks ! They will perforate something , maybe you should get paid to go and educate your fellow colleagues . Please please !
I am currently curled up in the foetal position with my womb in acidic fire pain 4 months post lap. Wish I had seen this sooner
I've had every exam imaginable and still not endo. My mum and nan both had hysterectomies in their 20s. I'm 37 and struggling all month long

I had this surgery to be told after there’s no evidence of endometriosis however they found adhesions in my pouch of Douglas…. How though? What would cause adhesions in this area 🤔 they make me feel like I’m going crazy!
I’ve had 2, first one it was seen and treated, 2nd and they said there wasn’t anything found! I know for a fact there is because the pain is horrific daily… and now this makes so much sense!!!!
ive got an obliterated pouch of Douglas (and it's everywhere else)

I got diagnosed in 2014, had 2 laps, saw pics of it everywhere, like red splodges and scar tissue. I'm 1 week post surgery and I go "no evidence of endo". saw pics its all pink, no dots only had 1 filmy adhesion. Can someone explain this please?

question i had 2 mris and multiple ultrasound and a pain mapping scan all show i had a frozen pelvis ovaries pulled to the back they were immobile the pouch of Douglas looked like it had advance endometriosis. I also have adenomyosis so when they went in to do my hysterectomy none of that was there there was a few adhesions but nothing like the scans said 🤷‍♀️😭
I was given lots of photos after my surgery. Pouch of Douglas included.
